topic: Provision containers using Azure Container Instances (ACI)
key_facts: Access takes places over HTTPS, using TLS to secure client connections.

Important

 Azure Container Instances requires all secure connections from servers and applications to use TLS 1.2.
Storing subscription IDs is required to ensure your container groups continue running as expected.
This enables you to combine your main application container with other supporting role containers, such as logging sidecars.
This is the only supported configuration for outbound connectivity from your container group in a virtual network.
Confidential containers on ACI are supported as a SKU that you can select when deploying your workload.

Storing credentials in plain text is a security risk; Microsoft advises customers to store user credentials in CLI environment variables to ensure they're encrypted/transformed when stored in the backend.
